chunk_summary: This section outlines tiered communication policies for Indian tele-nutrition, emphasizing safety, privacy, and clinical standards in low-bandwidth settings, with safeguards for child data and shared-phone households.
learning_objectives: Understand tiered communication channels for tele-nutrition in India based on bandwidth and clinical needs.
Learn safeguards for child data and shared-phone households in tele-nutrition practices.
Explore ethical considerations in using consumer messaging platforms for clinical purposes.
key_facts: Tier 1: Clinic platform messaging plus video for optimal care when available.
Tier 4: Low-tech care using phone counseling and scheduled in-person measurements.
Verify caregiver phone numbers at every care episode to prevent misdirected messages.
Explicit consent is required before receiving child images or feeding videos.
Child media must be uploaded to clinic repositories with role-based access and audit logs.
reference_quote: Verify the caregiver phone number at every episode of care to prevent misdirected messages in shared-phone households. (Source: Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, Reference 1)
document_key_topics: Safeguards for child data and shared-phone households
Ethical use of consumer messaging platforms
